title: Fondor shipyard assault
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Fondor}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle of Dubrillion (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Dubrillion]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' |conc= |next=[[Mission to Imdaar]]The Star Dreadnought that was being developed over Fondor was mentioned to be a prototype. As the ''[[Terror]]'', another ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought was shown with cloaking capabilities, was present in ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', this mission had to have taken place before the ''Terror''{{'s}} destruction during the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] development project. |image=[[File:Rebelstrike27.jpg]] |name=Fondor shipyard assault |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] |date=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] |place=[[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] ([[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|Fondor Shipyards]]) |result=*Decisive [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] victory *Destruction of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] prototype Super Star Destroyer |side1=Alliance to Restore the Republic |side2=Galactic Empire |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] *[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] |commanders2=[[Imperial officer/Legends|Facility officer]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*3 [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]] *10 [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] *5 [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]] *1 [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] *3 stolen [[TIE/hu "Hunter" Multi-Role Starfighter|TIE Hunters]] *1 [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Fondor)|GR-75 medium transport]] |forces2=*[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] *[[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] *6 TIE Hunters *4 [[Imperial escort carrier (storm commando)|Imperial escort carriers]] *1 docked [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] *Fondor facility turbolasers |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Some starfighters''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' — Assuming enough survive to finish the mission *The [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] {{C|[[Ramming/Legends|intentional]]}} |casual2=*Facility commander *Several TIE fighters *One Imperial escort carrier and its crew *[[Unidentified Executor-class Star Dreadnought|Prototype Star Dreadnought]] |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|This victory only ensures that they will be overconfident in the upcoming battle. Even now, the Rebels are being drawn into a trap.|Emperor Palpatine in response to the loss of the Star Dreadnought|}} Shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] launched the '''Fondor shipyard assault''' against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|Fondor Shipyards]]. The Rebels's plan, spearheaded by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], to destroy a [[Unidentified Executor-class Star Dreadnought|prototype]] [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]], was to utilize three previously stolen [[TIE/hu "Hunter" Multi-Role Starfighter|TIE Hunters]] and a reprogrammed [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Fondor)|GR-75 medium transport]] to infiltrate the shipyards. When their cover was blown, a Rebel fleet commanded by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] came out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and stormed the facility. When the shipyard defenses were disabled, the attack on the Super Star Destroyer commenced. Three [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking devices]] installed in the ship were targeted by the Rebel force, which caused a chain reaction of explosions which would compromise the entire starship. Though the Rebels emerged victorious, it was not as it seemed on the surface. Like all the other events surrounding the Rebel's assault on the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]], the events of the battle were manipulated by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. His decision to give the Alliance a major victory was based on giving the Alliance the courage it needed to mount their attack on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], which would have led to their destruction if Antilles and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] hadn't destroyed the Death Star II in time. ==Prelude== Following the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] escape from the [[Battle of Dubrillion (Galactic Civil War)|surprise ambush]] over [[Dubrillion/Legends|Dubrillion]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the elite Rebel flight [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], accompanied by two wingmen, somehow managed to steal a trio of experimental [[TIE/hu "Hunter" Multi-Role Starfighter|TIE Hunters]], elite TIE craft used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Storm commando/Legends|storm commando]] units. In retaliation for the massive losses over Dubrillion, Rogue group planned to destroy a unique Star Dreadnought with advanced [[Fondor Cloaking Device|cloaking device]] technology that was under construction in orbit of the industrial planet [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], after having somehow learned of its development. To achieve this task, the stolen TIE hunters were utilized, along with a "captured" [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Fondor)|Rebel transport]]. In reality, the transport was empty, and was programmed to [[Ramming/Legends|ram]] into one of the shipyard's [[Imperial Shield Station|shield stations]]' command deck to allow a Rebel [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] commanded by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] safe passage into the construction yard. ==The battle== [[File:Fondorex.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] with escorting Rebel fighters fly towards the Imperial control station.]] {{Quote|The transport has been programmed to ram into one of the primary shield stations!|General Crix Madine|}} When the reprogrammed transport was escorted into the perimeter of the shipyard, Antilles insisted to the base's commander that they were bringing in a shipment of prisoners. However, the Imperial commander quickly realized that it was a ruse both due to no scheduled appearance of prisoners and the lack of lifeforms being revealed in a scan of the "transport," and deployed [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] and an [[Imperial escort carrier (storm commando)|Imperial escort carrier]] to stop the rebels. Despite this, Rogue group still managed to defend the transport, which successfully plowed into the Imperial command center. A Rebel Nebulon-B escort frigate commanded by Madine, along with several starfighter squadrons, arrived to cover the attack on the generator. The generator's doors were deactivated by Antille's ion cannons. Meanwhile, his wingmen exchanged fire with several defensive turrets to give him cover as the frigate and its fighter complement exchanged fire with two additional Imperial escort carriers which had joined the battle. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike}} Eventually seeing no further use for the TIE Hunter, he then proceeded to dock with the frigate and utilize an [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]] for the remainder of the battle. {{Endgame}} He was quickly able to destroy the generator, shutting down the shield. More Rebel [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]], and A-wing [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] arrived, covering Antilles as he moved into the entrenched structures on the ship. Avoiding or destroying defensive [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] towers, Antilles and the others flew through the trenches, destroying the three cloaking devices installed in the ship, which caused a chain reaction. The result was that the entire Star Dreadnought exploded before Imperial reinforcements arrived.''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' ==Aftermath== {{Quote|The Rebels have destroyed the cloaking device on Fondor.
This victory only ensures that they will be overconfident in the upcoming battle. Even now, the Rebels are being drawn into a trap.
Yes, master.|Vader debriefs Emperor Palpatine on the Rebels' attack on the Fondor Shipyards|}} Though this was a clear victory for the Rebel Alliance, the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] believed it would make the Rebels overconfident in the upcoming battles. As he saw it, even then, the Rebels were being lured into a [[Battle of Endor/Legends|trap]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Using captured Imperial TIE fighters, launch a sneak attack on the Imperial shipyards of Fondor.|Mission briefing for "Fondor Shipyard Assault"|}} The battle over Fondor was depicted in the final mission of the Wedge Antilles campaign of the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''. If the player fails to destroy all cloaking generators in time, a cutscene will play where two ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers will warp into the system, presumably having been dispatched by the Imperial forces within the Fondor Shipyard as backup. The [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide|strategy guide]] for ''Rebel Strike'' referred to the initial plans of the Rebel Alliance to shut down the shield generator as being a variant of the Trojan Horse ploy,''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' ; "The Rebel Alliance has devised a variation of the Trojan Horse ploy. Posing as an Imperial pilot (in a TIE hunter), you will escort a booby-trapped transport ship to an Imperial target then attack an exposed shield generator." a reference to the real-life Greek-Trojan war, where the Greeks, as a final resort, proceeded to give a gift to the Trojans, a giant wooden horse when "surrendering" to them. The horse, known as the Trojan Horse, secretly contained a platoon of Greek Soldiers to infiltrate the city after the celebrations and kill the Trojans, also resulting in more Greeks arriving to infiltrate the city upon the destruction of the Trojan wall. Shortly after disabling the shield generator doors, the player has the option of switching out with an A-wing. The strategy guide mentions that doing so will make the final objective more challenging, although this article assumes the player did so. Some of the cutscene stills in the ''Rebel Strike'' strategy guide (which at the beginning of every coverage of a mission/campaign shows a film reel that depicted stills of cutscenes from the game or the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films) indicated that, during the level's creation, Antilles' wingmen would have originally piloted [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]] for the mission. They piloted TIE Hunters in the final version, like Antilles did. Unlike most other levels, the "Fondor Shipyard Assault" level was not given an audio commentary dedicated to it. The audio commentary for the level instead focused on the multiplayer option for ''Rebel Strike''. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Fondor shipyard assault}} {{Reflist}} {{GCW 3 ABY 2}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Fondor (4 NSY) |es=Asalto de los astilleros de Fondor }} [[Category:Battles of the Fondor Shipyards]] [[Category:Battles of the Galactic Civil War]]
